cases.notes: The document stays unchanged with two identical duplicate blocks; the observed query selects the first copy while the current target query selects the later copied duplicate, leaving the current domain at two exact-byte matches.

The observed query selects the first of two identical duplicate blocks separated by intervening content; the current document removes that first duplicate and the intervening block so the later duplicate becomes the only exact-byte survivor shifted into the same canonical block locator.

cases.notes: This is the explicit false-conflict case for document-wide binding when the target bytes are unchanged.
Any intermediate drift is irrelevant once the exact observed bytes return before reread.
The JSON string must decode directly to CRLF bytes; no second escape decoding is allowed.
Byte identity must survive multibyte UTF-8 scalars without normalization or char-count slicing.
